title: Launch Your Career in Civic Engagement: Apply for the CIVICUS Communications & Network Engagement Internship
summary: Are you passionate about civil society, citizen activism, and positive social change? CIVICUS, a global alliance of over 15,000 members across 189 countries, is looking for a Communications and Network Engagement Intern to join their team. This internship offers a unique opportunity to contribute to a worldwide mission defending people power and amplifying excluded voices, especially from the Global South. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); As a Communications and Network Engagement Intern, you will play a vital role in supporting CIVICUS global engagement through digital platforms. Your responsibilities will include creating compelling digital content, engaging with networks and membership, and collaborating with communicators across sectors to foster civic action. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys blending creativity, analytics, and relationship-building in a meaningful context. Key Responsibilities: Develop creative digital content for LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok. Strengthen network engagement with CIVICUS Narratives Network and members. Build relationships with communicators across civil society sectors. Support initiatives that amplify citizen activism and civic participation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Eligibility Requirements: Degree or equivalent experience in communications, marketing, social media, creative content development, or a related field. Excellent command of English; knowledge of a major Global South language is a plus. Experience in social media content creation, management, and analytics. Commitment to positive social change and civil society work. Strong interest in community engagement and civic action communications. How to Apply: Applications close 5th March 2026 at 11:59 PM SAST. Your application should include: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); A detailed CV including education, professional qualifications, significant positions, responsibilities, and achievements. A one-page cover note explaining your motivation for applying. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted within two weeks of the closing date. All applicants will be updated once the position has been filled. CIVICUS reserves the right to withdraw any vacancy at any time. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); This internship is an excellent pathway for emerging communicators and social change makers looking to gain hands-on experience in global civic engagement and digital network strategy. Deadline : 5th March,2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more job opportunities, click here . title: WFPG Summer 2026 Internship in Washington, D.C.: Programming, Communications, and Operations Roles Now Open
summary: Applications are now open for the WFPG Summer 2026 Internship Program, offering students and emerging professionals the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in programming, communications, and operations. Hosted fully in person in Washington, D.C., this competitive internship provides practical exposure within a leading organization focused on global policy and leadership. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The program is organized by the Women s Foreign Policy Group (WFPG), a respected nonprofit dedicated to advancing women s leadership in international affairs. About the WFPG Summer Internship Program The WFPG Summer 2026 Internship offers three distinct tracks: Programming Internship Communications Internship Operations Internship Applicants may apply for one or more internship tracks depending on their interests and qualifications. The program is designed to provide immersive, real-world experience in nonprofit operations and global policy engagement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); All internships are conducted fully in person at WFPG s Washington, D.C. location, allowing interns to work closely with professionals in the field of foreign policy and international leadership. Internship Tracks Overview Programming Internship Interns support event planning, research, and coordination of high-level discussions, panels, and leadership initiatives. This track is ideal for candidates interested in international affairs, public policy, and event management. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Communications Internship Interns assist with content creation, social media management, writing, and digital outreach. This role is suitable for individuals interested in communications strategy, media engagement, and policy messaging. Operations Internship Interns contribute to administrative processes, organizational management, and internal coordination. This track is valuable for those looking to build experience in nonprofit management and operations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Why Apply? The WFPG internship program offers: Hands-on experience in a respected foreign policy nonprofit Direct exposure to international affairs programming Networking opportunities in Washington, D.C. Practical skill development in communications, operations, and policy engagement A professional in-person work environment Interns gain valuable experience that strengthens graduate school applications, policy careers, nonprofit leadership pathways, and communications roles. Location and Format Location: Washington, D.C. Format: Fully in-person Term: Summer 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Candidates must be able to work on-site in Washington, D.C. for the duration of the internship. Application Deadline Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until March 6th at 11:59 PM ET, or until 100 applications are received, whichever comes first. Early applications are strongly encouraged due to the application cap. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Applicants should prepare their resume, cover letter, and any additional required materials before applying.
